title: Claim / verdict record
description: One CLAIM/VERDICT record per line, stored in a SEPARATE file from the execution ledger (.agent-proof/<run>/ledger.jsonl). These records describe what an agent CLAIMED and how that claim relates to the execution receipts — they are NOT execution proof. Keeping them in their own file (and out of ledger.jsonl) is the structural guarantee that an asserted claim can never be counted as a re-executed check; the execution readers (assert, verify, stop-gate) consume only ledger.jsonl and additionally reject any schema_version>=2 ledger line whose disposition is not reexecuted. This schema is reserved for the audit feature (a later release); the verdict vocabulary it will use is defined there.

label.description: The check label the claim refers to (joins to an execution receipt by label).

schema_version.description: Record format version, shared with the execution envelope. Claim/verdict records are 2+.

disposition.description: How the claim's evidence was handled. "asserted": the agent stated an outcome with no re-executed receipt behind it. "unparsed": a claim-shaped statement that could not be bound to a check. The "reexecuted" disposition is reserved for EXECUTION receipts in ledger.jsonl and never appears here.

claim_source.description: Where the claim came from, e.g. a structured claim marker the agent emitted, or a conservative transcript heuristic (tagged as inferred). Set by the audit feature.

parser_version.description: The claim parser/version that produced this record, so a verdict can be reproduced against the same extraction logic.

at.description: UTC time the claim was recorded (ISO 8601).

epoch.description: Unix epoch seconds when the claim was recorded.

session.description: Agent session id when the harness exports AGENT_DONE_SESSION; empty otherwise.
